data.title: Slovak Prime Minister Fico Warns of Potential EU Loan Block to Ukraine Amid Pipeline Dispute
data.description: Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico has issued a warning that Slovakia, alongside Hungary, may block European Union loans to Ukraine if the ongoing dispute over the 'Friendship' oil pipeline continues. According to Jin10, the disagreement centers around the pipeline, which is a crucial conduit for oil supplies in the region. Fico's statement underscores the potential geopolitical ramifications of the dispute, as it could impact EU financial support to Ukraine. The situation highlights the complex dynamics within the EU regarding energy security and financial aid policies.

data.title: Renewable Energy Investors Near Acquisition of Spanish Government Property in London
data.description: Renewable energy investors are advancing in their efforts to acquire a property owned by the Spanish government in a prestigious area of London. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the progress in negotiations that could lead to the transfer of ownership. The property, located in a sought-after district, has attracted significant interest due to its prime location and potential for development. This move is part of a broader trend where investors are increasingly targeting high-value real estate assets in major cities. The acquisition, if finalized, would mark a significant step for the investors in expanding their portfolio in the European market.

data.title: Energy Diversification Gains Importance Amid Middle East Crisis
data.description: EDP CEO Miguel Stilwell d’Andrade emphasized the significance of energy diversification in light of the Middle East crisis. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting d’Andrade's remarks on the increasing demand for renewable energy sources. He noted that renewables are more resilient to external shocks, underscoring their growing importance in the global energy landscape.

data.title: Israeli F-35I Fighter Downs Iranian Jet Over Tehran
data.description: An Israeli F-35I fighter jet has reportedly shot down an Iranian Yak-130 multirole aircraft over Tehran, according to the Israel Defense Forces. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the incident as a significant development in the ongoing tensions between the two nations. The Israeli military has not provided further details on the engagement or the circumstances leading to the confrontation. This event marks a notable escalation in the region's military activities, reflecting the complex geopolitical dynamics at play.

data.title: Live Nation (LYV) Stock Falls Premarket as DOJ Antitrust Trial Begins
data.description:
LYV shares moved lower as the Department of Justice opened its antitrust case accusing Live Nation and Ticketmaster of monopolizing the live music industry.

data.title: Sweden's Central Bank Advises Citizens to Keep Cash Reserves for Emergencies
data.description: Sweden's central bank has recommended that all adults maintain a week's worth of cash at home to ensure they can purchase essential items like food and medicine during a crisis. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's advice as a precautionary measure in the event of war or other emergencies. This guidance comes amid growing concerns about potential disruptions that could affect electronic payment systems. The central bank's advice underscores the importance of preparedness in maintaining access to necessary goods and services during unforeseen situations.

data.title: Ghana's Inflation Rate Reaches Lowest Level in Nearly Three Decades
data.description: Ghana's inflation rate dropped to its lowest point in nearly thirty years in February. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant decrease in inflation, which marks a notable economic milestone for the country. The decline in inflation is attributed to various economic policies and measures implemented by the government to stabilize the economy. Analysts suggest that this reduction could lead to improved economic conditions and increased investor confidence in Ghana. The government continues to focus on maintaining economic stability and fostering growth, aiming to sustain this positive trend in the coming months.

data.title: Ukrainian Drone Boats Target LNG Tanker Near Malta
data.description: An LNG tanker named Arctic Metagaz was reportedly targeted by Ukrainian drone boats near Malta's territorial waters, as stated by Russia's Transport Ministry. Bloomberg posted on X that the incident highlights ongoing tensions in the region. Further details about the attack or its impact on the vessel were not immediately available. The situation underscores the persistent geopolitical challenges affecting maritime operations in the area.

data.title: Asian Markets Feel Impact of U.S.-Iran Tensions
data.description: Asian financial markets are experiencing significant effects from the ongoing tensions between the United States and Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the region's vulnerability to geopolitical conflicts. Traders in Asia are questioning the reasons behind the impact on their markets, as the situation continues to unfold. The conflict has led to increased volatility and uncertainty, affecting investor sentiment and market stability across the region.

data.title: Lamborghini announces it will accept Bitcoin payments.
data.description: Lamborghini, the top sports car manufacturer with a market capitalization of $20 billion, announced that it will accept Bitcoin payments. (The Bitcoin Historian)

data.title: South Korean authorities have set a 20% cap on major shareholders' ownership in cryptocurrency exchanges.
data.description: South Korean authorities have capped the shareholding of major shareholders in cryptocurrency exchanges at 20%. (Solid Intel)

data.title: Renewable Energy Demand Expected to Rise Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Portuguese utility company EDP anticipates increased demand for renewable energy due to rising oil and gas prices linked to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting EDP's outlook as the geopolitical tensions continue to impact global energy markets. The company believes that the current situation will accelerate the transition to cleaner energy sources, as countries seek alternatives to fossil fuels. EDP's assessment comes amid broader concerns about energy security and price volatility, which are prompting governments and businesses to explore sustainable solutions. The utility firm is positioning itself to meet the growing demand for renewables, aiming to capitalize on the shift towards greener energy. As the conflict persists, the focus on renewable energy is expected to intensify, driving investment and innovation in the sector.

data.title: Acter Technology Secures Major Engineering Project in Thailand
data.description: Acter Technology Company Limited, a subsidiary of Shenghui Integration, has recently received a significant project order from Peng Shen Technology (Thailand) Co., Ltd. According to Jin10, the project, named 'Thailand Peng Shen PA03 Power and Interior One-time Equipment Electrical Engineering,' is valued at THB 1,090,000,000, approximately RMB 242 million. This development marks a substantial business engagement for Acter Technology in Thailand, reflecting the company's expanding footprint in the region.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks surged in pre-market trading in the US, with MSTR rising 7.65%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, crypto stocks surged in pre-market trading in the US, with BMNR up 7.2%, SBET up 4.82%, MSTR up 7.65%, CRCL up 6.08%, and COIN up 6.57%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Bitcoin Holds Above $71K as U.S. Dollar Index Nears 3-Month High, Bullish or Bearish for BTC?
data.description: Key takeawaysBitcoin is trading above $71,000, showing resilience despite a stronger U.S. dollar.The U.S. Dollar Index has climbed to around 99.4, approaching a three-month high.Bitcoin’s correlation with the Nasdaq-100 has dropped sharply, signaling possible market decoupling.$1.5 billion in Bitcoin ETF inflows in the past week highlights continued institutional demand.Bitcoin Trades Above $71K Despite Rising DollarBitcoin is holding above $71,000, maintaining strong momentum even as the U.S. dollar strengthens across global currency markets.The U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) recently climbed to 99.4, up from 96.6 just three weeks ago, as investors moved into cash and government bonds amid geopolitical uncertainty.Historically, a stronger dollar tends to pressure risk assets such as cryptocurrencies. However, Bitcoin has continued to defend the $68,000–$70,000 support range, indicating strong buyer interest.At the same time:The Nasdaq-100 declined about 1%Gold fell 3.6%Bitcoin’s ability to rise while traditional assets weaken suggests growing resilience during macro uncertainty.Bitcoin Decoupling From Tech StocksMarket data shows that Bitcoin’s correlation with the Nasdaq-100 has dropped significantly in recent sessions.Previous correlation: ~92%Current 30-day correlation: ~69%The decline suggests Bitcoin may be decoupling from technology equities, which had heavily influenced crypto price movements throughout much of 2025.This shift could support Bitcoin’s evolving role as:Digital goldA macro hedge assetAn alternative monetary systemRather than behaving purely like a speculative tech-style asset.Institutional Demand Remains StrongInstitutional capital continues flowing into Bitcoin.Spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds recorded roughly $1.5 billion in net inflows over the past seven days, highlighting sustained demand from large investors.These inflows indicate that institutional buyers are accumulating Bitcoin despite macro headwinds, helping stabilize prices above the $70,000 level.Miner Selling Concerns Weigh on SentimentInvestor sentiment briefly weakened after a regulatory filing from MARA Holdings raised concerns that the miner could sell part of its Bitcoin reserves.The speculation fueled fears that other mining companies might follow similar strategies, especially as some miners have shifted focus toward AI data center infrastructure.However, MARA clarified that it may buy or sell Bitcoin periodically, which does not imply a large-scale liquidation of its holdings.Is a Strong Dollar Still Bearish for Bitcoin?While dollar strength often creates pressure for crypto markets, the current DXY level remains well below the 105–110 range seen between late 2024 and early 2025.This suggests the dollar’s recent rally may represent temporary consolidation rather than a long-term macro trend.Meanwhile, Bitcoin’s ability to hold above $71,000 despite geopolitical tensions and rising dollar strength suggests the asset is becoming more resilient to traditional macro forces.Key Level to Watch: $75KDespite the recent rally, analysts say Bitcoin must break above $75,000 to confirm a new bullish phase.Until that level is reclaimed:Macro indicators such as the U.S. Dollar Index may continue influencing sentimentETF flows and institutional demand will remain key driversTraders will monitor the $70K support zone closelyFor now, Bitcoin’s move above $71,000 while the dollar strengthens signals a potential shift in market dynamics, reinforcing its position as an emerging macro asset rather than purely a risk-on trade.

data.title: Market News: South Korea Halts Stock Trading as Kospi, Kosdaq Crash 10% Amid Middle East Crisis
data.description: Key takeawaysSouth Korea’s Kospi and Kosdaq indexes plunged more than 10%, triggering market circuit breakers.The crash followed escalating tensions in the Middle East and rising oil prices.Asian markets broadly declined, with Japan’s Nikkei and Topix down nearly 4%.Global equities reportedly lost over $3.2 trillion in value in four days, according to analysts.South Korea Activates Circuit Breakers After 10% Market CrashSouth Korea’s stock market was forced to halt trading after benchmark indexes plunged sharply during morning trading on Wednesday.Both the KOSPI and KOSDAQ dropped more than 10%, triggering automatic circuit breakers designed to stabilize extreme volatility.The sharp decline marks the worst trading session for Korean equities since August 2024, according to regional market reports.Circuit breakers temporarily pause trading to prevent panic selling during sudden market crashes.Asian Markets Slide as Global Risk-Off Sentiment SpreadsThe sell-off extended across major Asian markets as investors rapidly reduced exposure to risk assets.Major index performance on Wednesday:Nikkei 225: down nearly 4%TOPIX: down about 4%Hang Seng Index: down roughly 3%Shanghai Composite Index: down about 1.3%According to market strategists, investors began locking in profits and rotating into safer assets as geopolitical tensions intensified.South Korea was particularly vulnerable due to its heavy reliance on imported energy.The country imports roughly 94% of its oil, with about 75% coming from the Middle East, making its economy sensitive to energy supply disruptions.Oil Prices Surge After Strait of Hormuz DisruptionThe market panic followed escalating conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran.Concerns increased after Iran reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s most critical oil transit corridors.Since the airstrikes began on February 28:Brent Crude Oil surged 14% to around $82 per barrelWest Texas Intermediate jumped 12% to roughly $75 per barrelRising oil prices are increasing fears of inflation shocks and supply disruptions across Asia, where many economies depend heavily on imported energy.Trump Signals War Could Continue IndefinitelyThe conflict escalated further after remarks from Donald Trump suggesting the military campaign against Iran could continue for an extended period.Trump stated the United States has a “virtually unlimited supply” of weapons and warned that wars can be “fought forever.”The U.S. Navy may also begin escorting oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, according to statements from the administration.$3.2 Trillion Wiped From Global StocksAccording to crypto researcher SungHoon Lee, global markets have already lost around $3.2 trillion in value over the past four days.He described the event as a potential “black swan” geopolitical shock, comparing it to the 1973 oil crisis, which triggered a prolonged global market downturn.Despite the turmoil in traditional markets, the crypto market has shown relative stability.Total crypto market capitalization fell only about 0.5% to roughly $2.39 trillion, according to data from CoinGecko.Markets Face Uncertainty as Geopolitical Risks RiseThe sudden market crash highlights how sensitive global financial markets remain to geopolitical developments.With oil supply disruptions, escalating military tensions, and rising energy costs, investors are increasingly shifting toward safe-haven assets and cash positions while monitoring how the crisis unfolds in the coming days.

data.title: SNB's Antoine Martin Highlights Increased Currency Market Intervention Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description: Swiss National Bank (SNB) policymaker Antoine Martin has indicated that the central bank is more inclined to intervene in currency markets due to the prevailing geopolitical climate. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Martin's remarks on the SNB's strategic approach to managing currency fluctuations. The central bank's readiness to act reflects concerns over global political developments that could impact economic stability. Martin's comments underscore the SNB's proactive stance in safeguarding Switzerland's financial interests amid uncertain international dynamics.

data.title: Thai PM Anutin Charnvirakul Nears Government Formation After Election Results Certification
data.description: Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul is advancing towards establishing a new government following the certification of the February 8 election results by the country's election commission. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this development in Thailand's political landscape. The certification marks a crucial step in the political process, allowing Charnvirakul to move forward with coalition negotiations and government formation. This follows a period of political uncertainty in Thailand, where the election results were awaited to determine the future leadership and governance structure. The certification of the election results is expected to pave the way for stability and progress in the nation's political affairs.
